page.title: venous thromboembolism — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for venous thromboembolism: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for venous thromboembolism: 105 alterations and 135 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

gmi_articles.title: Air travel and venous thromboembolism: a systematic review.
Travel is associated with a nearly 3-fold higher risk for VTE, with a dose-response relationship of 18% higher risk for each 2-hour increase in travel duration.
Increasing levels of free thyroxine as a risk factor for a first venous thrombosis.
Levothyroxine administration is associated with altered fibrinolysis and coagulation, indicating it may increase the risk of venous thrombosis.
Prevention of recurrent venous thrombosis and post-thrombotic syndrome.
Raloxifene therapy increases risk of deep venous thrombosis and pulmonary embolism.
Sauna bathing reduces the risk of venous thromboembolism.
Thrombocytopenia and intracranial venous sinus thrombosis after "COVID-19 vaccine astraZeneca" exposure.
US case reports of cerebral venous sinus thrombosis with thrombocytopenia after Ad26.COV2.S vaccination.
Bisphenol S remodels red blood cell membrane lipids by altering plasma lipid levels, causing the risk of venous thrombosis.
Epigallocatechin-3-gallate exerts protective effect on epithelial function.
Pre-treatment with glycyrrhizin inhibited venous thrombosis.
Blueberry phenolics reduce gastrointestinal infection of patients with cerebral venous thrombosis.
Economic class syndrome: epidemiological features and preventive measures
Medical issues associated with commercial flights.
Use of hormones and risk of venous thromboembolism.

disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Without anticoagulation: DVT progresses to PE in 30-50%; massive PE has 30%+ mortality without treatment; spontaneous resolution of small PE in 30-40% but recurrence risk high
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: DOACs (rivaroxaban, apixaban): equal or superior efficacy to warfarin with 50% reduction in major bleeding; catheter-directed thrombolysis for large DVT: rapid thrombus resolution but unclear long-term benefit on PTS (ATTRACT trial); acute massive PE with hemodynamic compromise: systemic thrombolysis reduces mortality 55-65%
remission.gap_size: Moderate — DOACs have largely replaced warfarin; remaining gap is in chronic anticoagulation decision-making and post-VTE care
remission.barrier_detail: DOAC superiority is established but warfarin persistence remains common in patients with mechanical valves (where DOACs are contraindicated) and in some LMIC settings; duration of anticoagulation after unprovoked VTE is an individualised decision poorly standardised; post-thrombotic syndrome (PTS) prevention with compression is rarely implemented
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — betrixaban/edoxaban expanding in specific populations; 2020 Andexanet alfa (reversal agent for FXa inhibitors) established; DOACs first-line since 2012-2014 (EINSTEIN, AMPLIFY trials); warfarin standard since 1954
remission.outdated_treatments: Prolonged bedrest after DVT (early ambulation with compression superior; bedrest increases PE risk and is now contraindicated); warfarin for uncomplicated DVT/PE where DOAC appropriate (inferior ease of use, bleeding risk, no reversal agent needed); IVC filter placement as primary VTE treatment (filters do not treat existing clot; anticoagulation superior in most cases)
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Compression stockings for PTS prevention (ECS after DVT — IDEAL DVT trial suggests post-DVT compression reduces PTS 25-30%); aspirin for extended VTE prophylaxis after DOAC course (ASPIRE, WARFASA trials — significant recurrence reduction); early ambulation protocols
remission.frontier_signal: Anti-XI-a antibodies (abelacimab, milvexian — prevent clotting without bleeding risk; Phase 3 AZALEA-TIMI 71 underway); Factor XIa inhibition paradigm shift; AI-based VTE risk scoring; direct reversible thrombin inhibitors without anticoagulation-related bleeding
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.
